name: Highland Federal Savings And Loan Association
description_short: Community-owned bank established in 1961 offering mortgages, deposit accounts, and basic banking services to Cumberland County residents with FDIC insurance protection.
description_long:
Highland Federal Savings and Loan Association was established in January 1961 with the mission of helping people own their homes and secure their financial future through FDIC-insured deposit products. Operating continuously for over 60 years, the institution has grown from a small office in the Will-Nell building to a 10,000 square foot main facility at 106 South Main Street in Crossville, Tennessee, plus a branch office in the Village Green Mall at Fairfield Glade. The company remains the only home-owned and home-operated financial institution in the Cumberland County area.

Highland Federal offers a range of banking and lending products centered on mortgages as their primary specialty, alongside deposit accounts, savings products, and basic banking services. They provide debit cards with instant issue service, mobile banking applications, 24-hour ATM access, and customer service through multiple channels including phone and email. Their loan specialists (Lewis Taylor and Angela Pennington) focus on mortgage lending, while the institution maintains standard banking hours Monday-Thursday 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM and Friday 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM.

What distinguishes Highland Federal is their local ownership structure and 60+ year commitment to the specific Cumberland County community. As a mutually-owned savings and loan, all strategic decisions are made with customer interests as the stated priority rather than shareholder returns. They maintain FDIC insurance protection on deposits and operate as an Equal Housing Lender. Their personal service approach and willingness to evaluate products "from the customer's perspective" reflects a community-focused philosophy unusual among larger financial institutions.

However, Highland Federal appears to be a limited-service regional institution. The website provides minimal detail about mortgage products, rates, terms, or other loan offerings. There is no information about competitive rates, specific loan programs (FHA, VA, conventional), or whether they serve borrowers beyond their geographic area. The digital presence is modest, with limited product transparency. Consumers seeking detailed product comparisons, extensive service options, or out-of-area lending may need to contact them directly or seek larger institutions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Mortgage loans for home purchase and financing
FDIC-insured deposit accounts and savings products
Debit card replacement and PIN change services
Loan consultation and application through dedicated loan specialists
pros: FDIC-insured deposits providing account protection up to $250,000
Locally owned and operated since 1961 with deep community roots in Cumberland County
Instant issue debit card service for quick card replacement
Mobile banking app available for online account management
24-hour ATM access for convenient cash withdrawals
Equal Housing Lender status ensuring non-discriminatory lending practices
Dedicated loan specialists (Lewis Taylor and Angela Pennington) for mortgage inquiries
cons: Limited geographic service area focused primarily on Cumberland County, Tennessee
Minimal product details on website with no rates, terms, or loan program specifics disclosed
Restricted business hours (Monday-Friday, closing at 4:30 PM most days) limiting accessibility
Small institution with only 2 branch locations reduces convenience for wider market
No information about online application process, turnaround times, or competitive positioning
best_for: Cumberland County, Tennessee residents seeking local mortgage lending with personal service
Community members wanting FDIC-insured savings and checking accounts from a trusted local institution
Homebuyers preferring to work with neighborhood-based lenders who understand local real estate markets
Customers valuing personal relationships and direct contact with loan officers over online-only banking
diagnosis: Highland Federal is best for Cumberland County residents who prioritize local ownership, personal service, and community connection over comprehensive product selection or rate shopping. The main caveat is that this is a small regional institution with limited transparency about mortgage terms and rates online—borrowers must contact them directly for loan details and may face geographic restrictions.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Community-owned bank established in 1961 offering mortgages, deposit accounts, and banking services to Cumberland County residents with FDIC insurance.
fdic_official_name: Highland Federal Savings and Loan Association
